description: 'mirewu' marks a chosen limit on who may enter, observe, or know. A room, conversation, record, or stretch of time can be private even when nothing is hidden. 'kupe' (hide) concerns concealment, while 'kupela' (secret) concerns deliberately untold information. 'sonu' (alone) says who is present; privacy says who has access.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced 'm', carry the resonance through the nose, and move into high-front unrounded 'i'. Make a tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge and release into stressed true-mid front 'e'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then continue into high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: Closed lips begin 'mi' inside nasal resonance. The word then turns through the tapped 'r' and gathers into rounded 'wu'; that movement from close resonance to a rounded finish can suggest a boundary quietly drawn.
usage_notes: 'mirewu' can modify a noun or stand with 'nai' as a predicate. Its quality noun is privacy.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: 'mirewu' lets a closed door or withheld record state a limit on access without rejecting the person outside it.

semantic_domains.communication: chosen limits on access to a conversation, record, or information
semantic_domains.community: a personal or shared boundary respected by other people
semantic_domains.spatial: a place reserved from uninvited entry or observation
